description: Manifold invariants defining topological properties and basin structures. Each invariant class maps to a Rosetta shape whose geometry encodes the invariant's character.

invariant_classes.name: Euler Characteristic
invariant_classes.description: V - E + F = 2 for all closed convex polyhedra. The topological fingerprint that all five Platonic solids share.
invariant_classes.note: Euler characteristic is invariant under continuous deformation — it survives any homeomorphism.

invariant_classes.name: Genus
invariant_classes.description: Number of handles on a surface. Genus 0 = sphere (Platonic solids). Higher genus = more complex topology.

invariant_classes.name: Curvature Basin
invariant_classes.description: Regions of a manifold where Gaussian curvature concentrates. Basins act as attractors for dynamic flows.

attractor_basins.description: Stable configurations the manifold relaxes toward. Each Platonic solid is a curvature basin attractor.

transition_saddles.description: Unstable equilibria between basins. Shape transitions pass through saddle points requiring activation energy.
transition_saddles.constraint: ENERGY_CONSERVATION — transition cost equals curvature difference between source and target basin.
